data.content:
### Configure WebWorker Test Environment for Dexie Addons Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/test/integration/test-syncable-dexie-tests.html This JavaScript snippet sets up global variables `window.workerSource` and `window.workerImports` to configure a WebWorker test environment. It specifies the main worker script and lists the Dexie core library along with its Observable and Syncable addons to be imported within the worker. ```JavaScript window.workerSource = "worker.js"; window.workerImports = [ "../dist/dexie.js", "../addons/Dexie.Observable/dist/dexie-observable.js", "../addons/Dexie.Syncable/dist/dexie-syncable.js" ]; ``` -------------------------------- ### Clone and Setup Dexie.js Development Environment Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md This snippet provides the commands to clone your forked Dexie.js repository, navigate into it, install project dependencies using pnpm, build the project, and then globally link it using npm link (or yarn/pnpm link) to make it available for local application development. ```Shell git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/Dexie.js.git dexie cd dexie pnpm install pnpm run build npm link # Or yarn link or pnpm link --global depending on what package manager you are using. ``` -------------------------------- ### db.on('changes') Event Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/README.md Details the `db.on('changes')` event, which allows subscribing to all database modifications, regardless of whether they originate locally or from other browser windows. It specifies the parameters passed to the callback function upon event trigger. ```APIDOC db.on('changes') event: Description: Subscribe to any database changes no matter if they occur locally or in other browser window. Parameters: - changes: Array Description: Array of changes that have occured in database (locally or in other window) since last time event was triggered, or the time of starting subscribing to changes. - partial: Boolean Description: True in case the array does not contain all changes. In this case, your callback will soon be called again with the additional changes and partial=false when all changes are delivered. ``` -------------------------------- ### Start Development Server with npm Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/vue/README.md This command launches a local development server using Vite. It enables hot-reloading for source file changes and displays lint errors directly in the console, facilitating rapid development. ```bash npm run dev ``` -------------------------------- ### Hello World with Dexie.js (React and TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/README.md This snippet illustrates how to integrate Dexie.js into a React application using TypeScript. It defines an interface for data entities, declares the Dexie database, and uses `useLiveQuery` from `dexie-react-hooks` to display and add data within a React component. ```tsx import React from 'react';\nimport { Dexie, type EntityTable } from 'dexie';\nimport { useLiveQuery } from 'dexie-react-hooks';\n\n// Typing for your entities (hint is to move this to its own module)\nexport interface Friend {\n id: number;\n name: string;\n age: number;\n}\n\n// Database declaration (move this to its own module also)\nexport const db = new Dexie('FriendDatabase') as Dexie & {\n friends: EntityTable;\n};\ndb.version(1).stores({\n friends: '++id, age',\n});\n\n// Component:\nexport function MyDexieReactComponent() {\n const youngFriends = useLiveQuery(() =>\n db.friends\n .where('age')\n .below(30)\n .toArray()\n );\n\n return (\n <>\n

My young friends

\n
    \n {youngFriends?.map((f) => (\n
  • \n Name: {f.name}, Age: {f.age}\n
    • \n ))}\n
\n {\n db.friends.add({ name: 'Alice', age: 21 });\n }}\n >\n Add another friend\n \n \n );\n} ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Dexie Observable Addon using npm Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/dist/README.md This snippet demonstrates how to install the dexie-observable addon into your project using the npm package manager. The --save flag adds it to your project's dependencies. ```bash npm install dexie-observable --save ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Dexie Addon using Bower and Verify Files Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/dist/README.md This snippet shows how to install the Dexie package, which includes addons like dexie-observable, using Bower. It also includes a command to verify that the observable addon's distribution files are present in the bower_components directory. ```bash bower install dexie --save ls bower_components/dexie/addons/Dexie.Observable/dist ``` -------------------------------- ### Demonstrate Dexie liveQuery with CRUD operations Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/liveQuery/liveQuery.html This JavaScript code initializes a Dexie database, defines a 'friends' store, and uses `liveQuery` to observe changes to friends within a specific age range. It then performs a series of asynchronous CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations on the database, logging the results of the live query to the console. It showcases how `liveQuery` automatically reacts to database modifications. ```JavaScript { "imports": { "dexie": "https://unpkg.com/dexie@^3.2/dist/modern/dexie.min.mjs" } } import Dexie, { liveQuery } from 'dexie'; const db = new Dexie('MyDatabase'); db.version(1).stores({ friends: '++id, name, age', }); const friendsObservable = liveQuery(() => db.friends.where('age').between(50, 75).toArray() ); const subscription = friendsObservable.subscribe({ next: (result) => console.log('Got result:', JSON.stringify(result)), error: (error) => console.error(error), }); const sleep = (ms) => new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms)); await sleep(1000); console.log('1. Adding friend'); const friendId = await db.friends.add({ name: 'Magdalena', age: 54 }); await sleep(1000); console.log('2. Changing age to 99'); await db.friends.update(friendId, { age: 99 }); await sleep(1000); console.log('3. Changing age to 55'); await db.friends.update(friendId, { age: 55 }); await sleep(1000); console.log("4. Setting property 'foo' to 'bar'"); await db.friends.update(friendId, { foo: 'bar' }); await sleep(1000); console.log('5. Deleting friend'); await db.friends.delete(friendId); subscription.unsubscribe(); ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Project Dependencies with npm Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/vue/README.md This command installs all necessary project dependencies listed in the `package.json` file. It should be run once after cloning the repository to set up the development environment. ```bash npm install ``` -------------------------------- ### Link Dexie.js Development Version to Local App Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md After globally linking the Dexie.js development repository, this command allows you to symlink that development version into your application's node_modules/dexie directory. This enables real-time testing of changes made in the Dexie.js source within your application. ```Shell npm link dexie # Or yarn link dexie / pnpm link dexie depending on your package manager. ``` -------------------------------- ### Build Dexie Observable Addon from Source Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/dist/README.md This set of commands outlines the process to clone the Dexie.js repository, navigate to the observable addon, install its dependencies, and then build or watch for changes to the addon's source code. This is useful for development or if pre-built versions are not suitable. ```bash git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/Dexie.js.git cd Dexie.js npm install cd addons/Dexie.Observable npm run build # or npm run watch ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure WebWorker for Dexie.Observable Tests Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/test/integration/test-observable-dexie-tests.html This JavaScript code configures global variables `window.workerSource` and `window.workerImports` to enable WebWorker testing within the Dexie unit test environment. It specifies the main worker script and the necessary Dexie and Dexie.Observable library imports for the worker. ```JavaScript // Hack for making the WebWorker test behave also here... window.workerSource = "worker.js"; window.workerImports = ["../dist/dexie.js", "../addons/Dexie.Observable/dist/dexie-observable.js"]; ``` -------------------------------- ### Include Dexie Syncable Libraries in HTML Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md Example of including Dexie, Dexie Observable, Dexie Syncable, and an example sync protocol implementation via script tags in an HTML file for browser-based usage. ```html ... ``` -------------------------------- ### Install Dexie.Syncable via npm Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/dist/README.md This command installs the Dexie.Syncable addon package from the npm registry. The --save flag adds it as a dependency to your project's package.json file, making it a required package for your application. ```shell npm install dexie-syncable --save ``` -------------------------------- ### Hello World with Dexie.js (Vanilla JavaScript, Legacy Script Tags) Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/README.md This example shows a 'Hello World' implementation using Dexie.js in vanilla JavaScript, loaded via a traditional script tag. It covers database declaration, adding data, and querying, using `.then()` for asynchronous flow. ```html \n\n \n \n \n \n ```
